column2: MAFFL will consist of 2 espn.com leagues and player pools, the 'MAFFL Upper-Tier' league and the 'MAFFL Lower-Tier' league
Seasonal Entry Fee and Prizes, along with the new Credit system are common to all MAFFL owners regardless of league placement
The 2 leagues will have an identical rule set, the only exceptions being league size, divisions and post-season play
Each season, 3 teams from the upper-tier will be relegated and 3 teams from the lower-tier will be promoted (see 'Promotion/Relegation Overview' tab)
In-Season game rules will adhere to the ESPN League Settings (ESPN.com)
Following the 2024 placements, all new owners will enter in the Lower-Tier league.
We will fill unexpected Upper-Tier league openings with the highest Credit total from the lower-tier league (tiebreaker: the highest ranking team from previous season).
The commissioner has final say on any disputes but will consider league votes for fairness.

column2: We will utilize espn.com platform to facilitate our league auctions
Each team is assigned a $200 budget.  Minimum bid = $1.   Maximum bid = Team Cap (($200 - Amount spent) - (Remaining roster spots to be filled))
See espn.com for more auction draft specific rules

Effective 2025: The Kicker (K) roster position has been permanently eliminated from the auction draft. All teams carry one fewer roster spot as a result. Roster sizes: 25 picks (2005), 16 picks (2006-2008), 17 picks (2009-2024), 22 picks (2025+).
Applies only when the Lower-Tier has an odd number of teams. Upper-Tier is fixed at 12 teams and is unaffected.
A placeholder team called 'MAFFL Ghost' is added to the Lower-Tier ESPN league to create an even schedule. MAFFL Ghost carries no roster.
Each week, the commissioner totals all real teams' scores, divides by the number of real teams, and applies that average to MAFFL Ghost as a scoring adjustment in ESPN LM Tools.
The real team matched up against MAFFL Ghost that week wins if they beat the average and loses if they don't.
MAFFL Ghost does not draft, earn credits, win prizes, or affect promotion/relegation.
All real teams remain eligible for weekly high score prizes every week, including the team facing MAFFL Ghost.

Example: Nine Lower-Tier teams score a combined 1,038 points in a given week. MAFFL Ghost score = 1,038 / 9 = 115.3. The team facing MAFFL Ghost that scored 119 wins. A team that scored 108 would have lost.
Entry Fee: Each team pays $100 per season to participate
Total Prize Pool: Calculated as $100 × total number of teams across both leagues
67% (2/3) of the total prize pool goes to the Upper-Tier League.
33% (1/3) of the total prize pool goes to the Lower-Tier League.
Upper-Tier Prize Distribution (67% of Prize Pool)

column2: Season Prizes Awarded / Season Credits Issued
Window to spend credits on division realignment
The credit system creates a new league currency,  issued to owners based on each team performance over a season and across multiple seasons.
Credits are awarded for both overall season outcomes and specific in-season achievements (bonus points).
The credit system does not directly affect promotion or relegation.
Both upper-tier and lower-tiers are rewarded on a similar scale, ensuring fairness regardless of the league level.
Credit balance carries weight and earned credits can be spent for competitive perks
The 'Owner Credit Balance' tab will serve as the source of truth when utilizing the Credit Balance for tiebreaking purposes
All Credit transactions will be logged in the 'Credit Log' tab
All credits will be awarded at the end of the season
Each season’s total (Outcome Credits + Bonus Credits) is added to a running cumulative credits total.
Credit Balance serves as the tiebreaker for all division, playoff and end-of-season seeding
Determines annual division alignment for Upper-Tier League (1/6/12 ; 2/5/11; 3/4/10; 7/8/9) (Note: tiebreakers determined by previous season W/L%, then total pts)
Determines Upper-Tier placement if unexpected openings occur
Credits are earned and can be spent (see 'Credit Overview' tab)
Credits can be traded directly or packaged with players.  (These trades adhere to the normal trade policy and deadlines)

column3: Finishing Position / Method
1st in playoffs (Champ!)
2nd in playoffs (Runner-Up!)
Eliminated in semifinals (reward for 1 playoff win)
Eliminated in quarterfinals (reward for playoff appearance)
7th overall - secured upper-tier status without a playoff run (made 7 vs 8 match)
8th overall - secured upper-tier status without a playoff run (made 7 vs 8 match)
Winning Relegation Showdown - win a challenge to remain in the upper tier

column2: Relegation Showdown Loser / Consolation Game Winner
column3: Winning consolation vs #12 or losing Relegation Showdown (relegated)
Finishing position 12 / losing consolation vs #11 (no points; team is relegated)
Winning #1 vs #2 Promotion Consolation Match - direct promotion to the upper tier
Losing #1 vs #2 Promotion Consolation Match - direct promotion to the upper tier
Finishing positions 3-4 & winning Promotion Showdown - wins the challenge to move up
Finishing positions 3-4 but losing Promotion Showdown - reward for a strong season even in a loss
Finishing positions 5+ - remains in the lower tier
Criteria
Highest cumulative team score in the season (awarded to the team with the highest total score)
Highest score in any single week (awarded for each reg season wk x14)
Most points from a single player in a weekly performance (awarded once; highest wkly performance including playoffs/consolation/promotion/relegation)
Largest margin of victory in a single week (awarded once; regular season only)
Avoiding the lowest weekly score all season (awarded once; Survivor style with weekly elimination)
Perk Description
$20 added to your Free Agent Auction Budget (FAAB) balance (anytime in-season; follows normal 'reset' league settings once added)
Double your point total for one position for one week (declare to commish and opponent before 1st game of the week kickoff; NOT ELIGIBLE for MAFFL Upper-Tier playoffs)
Make a regular season swap of opponents - a 1-for-1 swap of teams (regular season only; division matchups not eligible; declare to commish and all parties before 1st game of the week kickoff)
Make a pre-season swap of divisional alignment - a 1-for-1 swap of teams (declare in off-season prior to MAFFL draft following division alignment on 8/1)

Guarantee your safety from relegation for the current season regardless of final standing (declare before Regular Season Week 4 1st game kickoff; ensures tiebreaker elevation to Seed #7 if unable to make Playoffs)
